name: General Tso's Chicken - Hom
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Rice wine or dry sherry
2 Dried red chiles, cut in half lengthwise
1 tablespoon Chopped fresh orange peel OR 2 teaspoons Dried citrus peel (soaked and coarsely chopped)
1/2 teaspoon Roasted Sichuan peppercorns (finely ground), optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into thin slices, about 2 inches long, cutting against the grain. Place it in a bowl with the soy sauce, rice wine or sherry, ginger, cornstarch, and 1 tablespoon sesame oil. Mix well and let the mixture marinate for about 20 minutes.
2. Heat the oil in a wok or large skillet until very hot. Remove the chicken from the marinade using a slotted spoon and add it to the pan. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until it browns.
3. Remove the chicken and leave it to drain on a colander or sieve. Pour off most of the oil, leaving about 2 tablespoons.
4. Reheat the pan over high heat and then add the dried chiles. Stir-fry for 10 seconds.
5. Return the chicken to the pan. Add the remaining ingredients and stir-fry for 4 minutes, mixing well.
6. Serve the dish immediately.
